_notes: Sources: r/goth wiki, community research, verified press reports.
tier: 'convicted' = legal conviction on record (shows RED in browser)
tier: 'accused'   = credible allegation, no conviction or acquitted (shows YELLOW)
tier: 'settled'   = investigated/accused but closed with apology/no charges, community largely moved on (shows GREEN)
artists[]  = primary artist names OR band names this person records/recorded under
members[]  = bands whose tracks may include this person as member/collaborator
summary    = one-line note shown as tooltip at the DJ booth
song_flags = individual songs flagged (not whole artist) — handled separately in UI
Add/remove/edit freely. Server reloads on restart.

flags.name: Ian Watkins
flags.summary: Convicted 2013 — multiple child sexual abuse charges

flags.name: Varg Vikernes
flags.summary: Convicted 1994 — murder of Euronymous + arson of multiple churches

flags.name: Bård Eithun
flags.summary: Convicted 1994 — murder (Lillehammer homophobic attack)

flags.name: Dustan Sheehan
flags.summary: Convicted — CP possession and attempted grooming of a minor (Batcave Records)

flags.name: Tobias Francis
flags.summary: Convicted — domestic battery (2020s); frontman of Soft Kill

flags.name: Michel Rowlands
flags.summary: Band member convicted of rape — confirmed via band's own public statement

flags.name: Till Lindemann
flags.summary: 2023 allegations of systematic sexual exploitation at shows; German prosecutors closed Aug 2023 — insufficient evidence; Lindemann sued Der Spiegel over reporting

flags.name: Alison Moyet
flags.summary: Signed anti-trans letter attacking LGBT+ charity Stonewall, later withdrew support

flags.name: Drab Majesty
flags.summary: Multiple racist tweets; issued repeated apologies and donated to charity — widely accepted by the scene

flags.name: Siouxsie Sioux
flags.summary: Wore swastika armband on stage (1970s punk provocation); apologised; also see individual song flags for Arabian Nights, Hong Kong Garden

flags.name: Marilyn Manson
flags.summary: Multiple named accusers alleged sexual/psychological abuse (2021); Esmé Bianco civil suit settled 2023; LA DA closed criminal investigation Jan 2025 — statute of limitations

flags.name: Richard Ramirez (noise)
flags.summary: Noise artist — name overlap with Night Stalker serial killer; flagged for DJ awareness only, not a legal matter

flags.name: Boyd Rice
flags.summary: 1986 on-camera appearance on white supremacist TV promoting Aryan music agenda; photographed with neo-Nazi organizers; denies ideology, claims artistic provocation

flags.name: Douglas Pearce
flags.summary: Sustained Nazi-era imagery; expressed 'Eurocentric Racialism' in interviews; SPLC classifies as white power music; Pearce disputes Nazi label

flags.name: Mr. Kitty
flags.summary: Publicly admitted 2021 to sending explicit sexual content to a 15-year-old fan over 4 months in 2019; no criminal charges filed; returned to performing 2024

flags.name: And One
flags.summary: Official accounts posted QAnon and far-right conspiracy content 2020; blamed on a hacker — widely disbelieved; headlined M'era Luna 2025 without public reckoning

flags.name: Andy Laplegua
flags.summary: Fascist and misogynistic song content; Confederate flag displayed; bandmate Joe Letz did blackface — Laplegua did not publicly condemn it

flags.name: Al Jourgensen
flags.summary: Documented history of abusing women, detailed in his own autobiography; bandmate Sin Quirin accused of underage sexual relationships — band stood by him

flags.name: Morrissey
flags.summary: Longstanding racism and xenophobia; called Chinese people a 'subspecies' (2010); supports far-right UK parties; defended Kevin Spacey and Harvey Weinstein

flags.name: Jimmy Urine
flags.summary: Sued for sexual assault of a minor; civil case settled out of court 2024; no criminal conviction

flags.name: Andy Deane
flags.summary: Documented serial abuser — multiple partners; cheating and possible STD transmission; community-sourced evidence via Bella Verita project

flags.name: Adam Bravin
flags.summary: Abusive behaviour documented in relation to Cloak and Dagger Club LA; multiple victim accounts

flags.name: Genesis P-Orridge
flags.summary: Documented abuse of ex-wife; abuse as head of TOPY (Temple ov Psychick Youth); abuse of Cosey Fanni Tutti

flags.name: Nikolas Schreck
flags.summary: Nazi imagery and fascist ideology; longstanding association with Boyd Rice and white nationalist networks

flags.name: Ronny Moorings
flags.summary: 2021 album widely read as pro-QAnon; made dismissive jokes about Trump's border wall and #MeToo; posts AI-generated slop on social media

flags.name: Michale Graves
flags.summary: Former Misfits vocalist; testified on behalf of Proud Boys at Jan 6 sedition trial, stating they 'didn't mean to attack the Capitol'

flags.name: Thomas Rainer
flags.summary: Sustained use of Nazi-adjacent imagery; racist and misogynistic content; genre-community consensus leans toward genuine ideology rather than provocation

flags.name: Rozz Williams
flags.summary: Heavily into Nazi imagery throughout career; band (current members) also noted for COVID conspiracy theories

flags.name: Michael Gira
flags.summary: Accused of rape by singer Larkin Grimm (2016); Gira denied, issued statement — no charges filed; ongoing community debate

flags.name: Brian Graupner
flags.summary: Signal-boosting anti-trans and far-right spokespeople on social media; associated with other flagged musicians; dropped from Dark Force Fest 2025

flags.name: Jimmy Page
flags.summary: Well-documented statutory rape of 14-year-old Lori Maddox in 1970s; relevant to David Bowie-era groupie culture context

flags.name: David Bowie
flags.summary: Credible accounts of sex with underage groupies in the 1970s (Lori Maddox era); no charges; also see China Girl video for racist gesture

flags.name: Alice Cooper
flags.summary: Anti-trans public comments; dropped by makeup brand MAC in 2023

flags.name: Nina Hagen
flags.summary: TERF (trans-exclusionary radical feminist) public statements; AIDS denier

flags.name: Joolz Denby
flags.summary: Band manager and album artist for New Model Army; public transphobic statements

flags.name: Brandon Plybus
flags.summary: Active member of Sons of Confederate Veterans — racist and anti-LGBT confederate organization

flags.name: William Control
flags.summary: Accused 2018 of operating a coercive BDSM sex cult targeting fans; police declined to prosecute; wrote an album glorifying the abuse

flags.name: Ethan Kath
flags.summary: Accused of sexual assault and domestic abuse by former bandmate Alice Glass (2017); no charges filed; band effectively ended

flags.name: Matt Slowikow
flags.summary: Racist conduct documented via community screencaps

flags.name: Richard Jomshof
flags.summary: Band member is a Swedish anti-Muslim far-right politician (Sweden Democrats party)

flags.name: vISION
flags.summary: Far-right supporter; band performed in Nazi uniforms/swastikas at Utah Dark Arts Fest 2001

flags.name: Helios Creed
flags.summary: Racist statements on social media; remaining bandmates walked out in protest

song_flags.title: Romeo's Distress
song_flags.reason: Contains a racial slur (N-word) in the first verse

song_flags.title: Why Can't I Be You?
song_flags.reason: Music video opens with blackface imagery (0:02)

song_flags.title: China Girl
song_flags.reason: Music video contains racist 'Chinese eyes' gesture

song_flags.title: Hong Kong Garden
song_flags.reason: Racist lyrics (though original intent was reportedly anti-racist)

song_flags.title: Christfuck
song_flags.reason: Glorifies sexual abuse in lyrical content

song_flags.title: Don't Blow No Bubbles
song_flags.reason: Homophobia — song blames gay people for AIDS
